product: Change Owner of Streaming Tables and Materialized Views
tagline: Enhance ownership management for pipeline datasets in Databricks SQL.
what_it_does: The update allows users to change the owner of streaming tables and materialized views that are backed by standalone pipelines in Databricks SQL using the `ALTER STREAMING TABLE` and `ALTER MATERIALIZED VIEW` commands with `SET OWNER TO`. This functionality is applicable to datasets created with either Databricks SQL or Lakeflow Connect, enabling greater flexibility in dataset management. However, modifications made with `ALTER` statements may be overridden by the pipeline's SQL definition during updates.
why_it_matters: This enhancement streamlines the management of data ownership, which is crucial for governance and compliance in data operations. By enabling ownership changes directly through SQL commands, organizations can improve their data stewardship and reduce administrative overhead. This is particularly beneficial for teams managing multiple datasets and needing to reassign ownership as personnel or project responsibilities change.

capabilities.title: Change Owner
capabilities.desc: Use `SET OWNER TO` in SQL commands to update dataset ownership.

capabilities.title: Modify Streaming Tables
capabilities.desc: Alter properties of streaming tables created with standalone pipelines.

capabilities.title: Adjust Materialized Views
capabilities.desc: Change attributes of materialized views using SQL commands.

capabilities.title: Compatibility with SQL Environments
capabilities.desc: Utilize `ALTER` statements across various Databricks SQL environments.

prerequisites: User must have appropriate permissions to alter dataset ownership.
Datasets must be created using standalone pipelines in Databricks SQL.
use_cases: Reassign ownership of datasets when team members change.
Manage access control for sensitive data effectively.
Update dataset ownership as part of compliance audits.
limitations: Changes made with `ALTER` statements may be overridden by the pipeline's SQL definition during updates.
Cannot modify the schedule or trigger of a dataset defined in SDP with an `ALTER` statement.

steps.title: Access Databricks SQL
steps.desc: Log into your Databricks SQL environment.

steps.title: Identify Dataset
steps.desc: Locate the streaming table or materialized view you wish to modify.

steps.title: Execute ALTER Command
steps.desc: Run the `ALTER STREAMING TABLE` or `ALTER MATERIALIZED VIEW` command with `SET OWNER TO`.

steps.title: Verify Changes
steps.desc: Check the ownership of the dataset to confirm the update.

steps.title: Monitor for Conflicts
steps.desc: Be aware of potential overrides from the pipeline's SQL definition on updates.

key_takeaway: The ability to change ownership of streaming tables and materialized views enhances data governance and operational efficiency in Databricks SQL.
